sources: Silverstein, Webster, Kiemle - Spectrometric Identification of Organic Compounds

metadata.description: Comprehensive FTIR annotation rule database covering organic functional groups, thermosetting resins, and inorganic filler materials (v3.0.1)

changes.v2.0.0_to_v3.0.0: Added CCL inorganic filler rules (SiO₂, γ-AlOOH, Al₂O₃, Mg(OH)₂, Al(OH)₃) with experimental validation
changes.v3.0.0_to_v3.0.1: Added thermosetting resin functional groups: epoxy, cyanate ester, bismaleimide (BMI), thiol, isocyanate, ether, polyimide (PI)

ohStretching.name: O-H Stretching
ohStretching.description: Vibrational mode: O-H bond elongation and compression, 3000-3700 cm⁻¹ for organic, 3000-3800 cm⁻¹ for inorganic

primaryPeak.description: Hydrogen-bonded O-H stretch (most common)

alternativeForm.description: Free O-H (non-hydrogen-bonded, rare in neat samples)

subTypes.notes: Sharp, well-defined peak. No C=O at 1680-1750. Position higher than carboxylic acid O-H.

primaryPeak.description: Extremely broad O-H stretch, often centered around 3000 cm⁻¹, characteristic dull envelope

subTypes.notes: VERY BROAD peak is diagnostic. Peak position much lower (2500) than alcohols. Must have C=O stretch 1680-1720.

primaryPeak.description: O-H attached to aromatic ring, position lower than aliphatic alcohols

secondaryPeaks.note: Multiple peaks in aromatic region, distinct from aliphatic

subTypes.notes: Position intermediate between alcohol and carboxylic acid. MUST have aromatic C=C peaks. Sharper than carboxylic acid.

primaryPeak.description: Metal-coordinated O-H, often very broad due to hydrogen bonding network

secondaryPeaks.note: Position varies widely depending on metal coordination

subTypes.notes: Very broad, often multiple overlapping bands. Look for lack of C-H stretches above 2800 cm⁻¹.

primaryPeak.description: Two overlapping O-H stretches from symmetric and asymmetric modes

secondaryPeaks.note: Characteristic peak, distinct from C=O stretch

subTypes.notes: Look for combination with broad H-O-H bend at 1650. Often appears as contaminant.

primaryPeak.description: SINGLE DIAGNOSTIC PEAK - O-H stretch of Mg(OH)₂ brucite structure

subTypes.notes: HIGHLY CHARACTERISTIC: Single sharp peak at 3698 cm⁻¹ is diagnostic for Mg(OH)₂. No other filler shows this exact pattern. FWHM < 25 is key identifier.

primaryPeak.description: THREE DIAGNOSTIC SHARP PEAKS - O-H stretches of Al(OH)₃ gibbsite

peakDetails.label: Al-OH stretch (high-freq)
peakDetails.note: Most characteristic peak, distinguishes from boehmite

subTypes.notes: DISTINCTIVE PATTERN: Three sharp bands (NOT broad) vs. boehmite's two broad bands. Multiple sharp peaks is diagnostic. Often used as flame retardant (ATH) in polymers.

primaryPeak.description: TWO BROAD PEAKS - O-H stretches of boehmite structure

subTypes.notes: BROADER peaks distinguish from gibbsite (sharp). O-H peaks are broad (FWHM > 80). Unique Al-O stretching at 1066, 1156 cm⁻¹ is diagnostic. Thermal calcination converts to Al₂O₃ (loss of OH).

siOStretching.name: Si-O Stretching
siOStretching.description: Vibrational mode: Si-O bond in silicate minerals and SiO₂-based fillers

primaryPeak.description: Si-O-Si asymmetric stretching, strongest FTIR absorption for SiO₂

subCases.label: Fumed silica (aerosil, pyrogenic)
subCases.note: Higher frequency due to more surface silanols

subTypes.notes: Very broad and intense Si-O asymmetric stretch (1000-1100) + sharp symmetric peak at 800 cm⁻¹ = diagnostic SiO₂. No O-H from filler itself (organic). Often primary filler in CCL and epoxy formulations.

alOStretching.name: Al-O Stretching
alOStretching.description: Vibrational mode: Al-O bond in aluminum oxide and aluminate minerals

primaryPeak.description: Al-O stretching in broad complex pattern

diagnosticFeature.note: Calcined alumina shows NO O-H - complete dehydration distinguishes from hydroxide precursors

subTypes.notes: DIAGNOSTIC: Very broad Al-O absorption WITHOUT any O-H stretch. Indicates fully calcined (dehydrated) alumina. Used as high-temp filler. Broad pattern from AlO6 octahedral coordination.

chStretching.name: C-H Stretching
chStretching.description: Vibrational mode: C-H bond elongation and compression, typically 2800-3100 cm⁻¹

primaryPeak.description: Aliphatic C-H stretch, strong and sharp

subTypes.notes: Most common C-H stretch. Below 3000 cm⁻¹ is characteristic. Very strong, sharp appearance.

primaryPeak.description: sp² C-H stretch, appears ABOVE 3000 cm⁻¹

subTypes.notes: KEY FEATURE: Peak above 3000 cm⁻¹ indicates unsaturation. Always check for C=C stretch.

primaryPeak.description: Alkyne C-H stretch, very sharp and distinctive

subTypes.notes: Terminal alkyne: C-H very sharp near 3300. Internal alkyne: no C-H stretch.

primaryPeak.description: Aromatic C-H stretch, appears above 3000 cm⁻¹, often multiple peaks

subTypes.notes: Weak peaks above 3000. Look for aromatic C=C multiplets.

primaryPeak.description: Characteristic aldehyde C-H stretch, appears as TWO peaks (doublet)

secondaryPeaks.note: Very strong, characteristic position for aldehyde

subTypes.notes: DIAGNOSTIC: Doublet in 2830-2695 region is highly characteristic of aldehyde. Must have C=O 1740-1720.

coStretching.name: C=O Stretching
coStretching.description: Vibrational mode: C=O bond elongation and compression, strongest IR absorptions, 1630-1780 cm⁻¹

primaryPeak.description: Aldehyde C=O stretch, characteristic strong sharp peak

subTypes.notes: One of strongest absorptions. Combined with aldehyde C-H doublet = very high confidence.

primaryPeak.description: Ketone C=O stretch, aliphatic ketone near 1725 cm⁻¹

subTypes.notes: No O-H, N-H stretches. Two carbon groups attached.

primaryPeak.description: Carboxylic acid C=O stretch

subTypes.notes: Combination of very-broad O-H stretch + C=O in 1680-1720 = very high confidence carboxylic acid.

primaryPeak.description: Ester C=O stretch, typically higher than ketone

subTypes.notes: No O-H or N-H stretches. Higher C=O frequency than ketone.

primaryPeak.description: Amide C=O stretch (Amide I band), lowered due to resonance

subTypes.notes: C=O lowered by resonance compared to ketone/ester. Broad due to H-bonding.

nhStretching.name: N-H Stretching
nhStretching.description: Vibrational mode: N-H bond elongation and compression, 3300-3500 cm⁻¹

primaryPeak.description: Two N-H stretching bands: symmetric and asymmetric modes

subTypes.notes: TWO peaks is diagnostic for primary amine. Secondary has ONE, tertiary has NONE.

primaryPeak.description: Single N-H stretch band

subTypes.notes: ONE peak distinguishes from primary (two) and tertiary (none).

primaryPeak.description: Two N-H stretching bands of amide

subTypes.notes: Two N-H peaks + strong C=O at 1680-1690 = primary amide.

shStretching.name: S-H Stretching
shStretching.description: Vibrational mode: S-H bond elongation and compression, characteristic weak but sharp peak, 2550-2600 cm⁻¹

primaryPeak.description: S-H stretching, weak but sharp and characteristic

subTypes.notes: S-H stretch is diagnostic due to weak intensity and sharp appearance. Used in thiol-epoxy, thiol-yne systems for cross-linking.

epoxyStretching.name: Epoxy C-O Stretching & Ring Vibrations
epoxyStretching.description: Vibrational mode: C-O bonds in epoxide (oxirane) ring, characteristic pattern used to track epoxy conversion

primaryPeak.description: C-O-C stretching of epoxide ring, complex multiplet pattern

subTypes.notes: Multiple diagnostic peaks in 900-1250 cm⁻¹ region. Pattern is highly characteristic of epoxy resins. Used to monitor cure kinetics.

nitrileStretching.name: C≡N & N=C=O Stretching (Nitriles & Isocyanates)
nitrileStretching.description: Vibrational mode: Triple bonds and cumulative functional groups in 2100-2300 cm⁻¹ region

primaryPeak.description: N=C=O asymmetric stretching (cumulative N=C=O group)

subTypes.distinguishFrom: Alkyne C≡C (2100-2260) is sharper and weaker; isocyanate is broader and stronger
subTypes.notes: Characteristic signature for polyurethane precursors. Broader and stronger than alkyne due to larger dipole moment.

primaryPeak.description: O-C≡N stretching (cyanate ester functional group)

subTypes.notes: Similar wavenumber to isocyanate, but narrower and medium intensity. Used for aerospace applications.

imideStretching.name: Imide C=O Stretching (Polyimide)
imideStretching.description: Vibrational mode: C=O bonds in imide groups (N-C(=O)-C(=O)-N), diagnostic for polyimide polymers

primaryPeak.description: Imide C=O asymmetric stretching (high-frequency carbonyl)

subTypes.notes: Doublet pattern (1780 + 1720) is HIGHLY DIAGNOSTIC for imide. High thermal stability. Used in aerospace/electronics.

maleimidesStretching.name: Maleimide C=O Stretching (BMI)
maleimidesStretching.description: Vibrational mode: C=O stretching in maleimide (cyclic imide with C=C double bond), used in bismaleimide resins

primaryPeak.description: Maleimide C=O stretch (high-frequency carbonyl, part of 5-membered ring)

subTypes.notes: Doublet (1776 + 1718) distinguishes BMI from other imides. Higher temp capability than epoxy. Used in composites.

etherStretching.name: C-O Stretching (Ethers & Polyethers)
etherStretching.description: Vibrational mode: C-O single bond stretching in ether groups (R-O-R'), typically shows multiple intense bands

primaryPeak.description: Polyether C-O stretching, overlapping bands from many equivalent and non-equivalent C-O bonds

subTypes.diagnosticFeature: BROAD INTENSE REGION 1000-1300 with NO C=O at 1700 (distinguishes from polyesters)
subTypes.notes: Used in epoxy resins, polyurethanes, and polyols. Broad absorption due to chain conformations and multiple C-O environments.

fingerprint.name: Fingerprint Region & Combination Bands
fingerprint.description: Region below 1500 cm⁻¹ containing multiple overlapping bending, stretching, and combination bands

primaryPeaks.note: Number of bands indicates chain conformation and crystallinity

subTypes.notes: Simple spectrum dominated by C-H stretches.
Strong C-Cl at 1260 + trans-C=C wag at 960 are diagnostic.
Aromatic peaks + ester C=O + para-substitution pattern diagnostic.

sharp.note: Indicates strong bond with little coupling

broad.note: Hydrogen bonding or complex bonding environment

very-broad.note: Characteristic of strong hydrogen bonding network

crossValidationRules.title: Mg(OH)₂ Identification (Brucite)
crossValidationRules.rule: If single sharp peak @ 3698 cm⁻¹ present with FWHM < 25 and NO other O-H peaks, then HIGHLY CONFIDENT Mg(OH)₂. This single peak is DIAGNOSTIC - no other common filler shows this exact pattern.

crossValidationRules.title: Al(OH)₃ vs Boehmite Distinction
crossValidationRules.rule: If THREE SHARP O-H peaks @ 3620, 3530, 3400 cm⁻¹ (FWHM < 40 each) → Al(OH)₃ GIBBSITE. If TWO BROAD O-H peaks @ 3404, 3089 cm⁻¹ (FWHM > 80) → BOEHMITE. Additional: boehmite shows Al-O stretching @ 1066, 1156 cm⁻¹.

crossValidationRules.title: SiO₂ (Silica) Confirmation
crossValidationRules.rule: If very broad, intense Si-O asymmetric stretch @ 1000-1100 cm⁻¹ (FWHM 80-150) + sharp Si-O symmetric peak @ 800 cm⁻¹, then SiO₂ CONFIRMED. Absence of O-H confirms silica (not hydrated minerals).

crossValidationRules.title: Al₂O₃ (Calcined Alumina) Confirmation
crossValidationRules.rule: If very broad Al-O absorption @ 500-900 cm⁻¹ PLUS complete ABSENCE of O-H stretch (3000-3700), then Al₂O₃ CALCINED. This NO O-H feature distinguishes from hydroxide precursors (boehmite, gibbsite).

crossValidationRules.title: Inorganic Filler vs Organic Compound
crossValidationRules.rule: If strong absorptions in 400-1200 cm⁻¹ region (metal-O stretches) combined with absence of typical C-H stretches @ 2800-3100 cm⁻¹, then INORGANIC FILLER. Presence of distinct C-H bands indicates organic compound or polymer matrix.

crossValidationRules.title: Epoxy (Epoxide Ring) Identification
crossValidationRules.rule: If MULTIPLE peaks @ 1250 cm⁻¹ (C-O-C asymmetric), 913-917 cm⁻¹ (C-O deformation), and 830-863 cm⁻¹ (secondary C-O), then EPOXY RESIN. These diagnostic peaks distinguish epoxide from ester (which lacks 830-863 region). Use to monitor cure: epoxy peaks decrease as O-H peaks increase @ 3200-3400.

crossValidationRules.title: Polyimide (PI) Identification
crossValidationRules.rule: If DOUBLET of C=O peaks @ 1775-1780 cm⁻¹ (asymmetric) AND 1716-1722 cm⁻¹ (symmetric) PLUS C-N peak @ 1372-1396 cm⁻¹, then POLYIMIDE. This doublet pattern is DIAGNOSTIC for imide group. High thermal stability indicator.

crossValidationRules.title: Bismaleimide (BMI) Identification
crossValidationRules.rule: If DOUBLET of C=O peaks @ 1776-1765 cm⁻¹ (high-freq) AND 1718-1710 cm⁻¹ (low-freq), then BMI. Similar to polyimide but wavenumbers slightly higher (ring strain from 5-membered maleimide). C=C double bond may be visible @ 1600-1650. Higher temp resin than epoxy.

crossValidationRules.title: Isocyanate vs Alkyne Distinction
crossValidationRules.rule: If peak @ 2250-2280 cm⁻¹: Check FWHM and intensity. ISOCYANATE N=C=O is BROADER (FWHM 30-60) and STRONGER. ALKYNE C≡C is SHARPER (FWHM < 20) and WEAKER. Isocyanate peak @ 2268 cm⁻¹ is most common. Use to track polyurethane cure (isocyanate decreases as urethane forms).

crossValidationRules.title: Thiol (S-H) Detection
crossValidationRules.rule: If WEAK but SHARP peak @ 2550-2600 cm⁻¹, then THIOL (S-H group). Low intensity distinguishes from other stretches. Used in thiol-epoxy and thiol-yne click chemistry cross-linking systems. Less common than isocyanate/epoxy.

crossValidationRules.title: Cyanate Ester vs Isocyanate
crossValidationRules.rule: Both show peaks around 2250-2260 cm⁻¹. CYANATE ESTER (O-C≡N) is NARROWER (FWHM 20-40) and MEDIUM intensity. ISOCYANATE (N=C=O) is BROADER and STRONGER. Cyanate esters used for aerospace (better high-temp). Peak disappears during cure (replaced by aromatic C=N @ 1500-1600).

crossValidationRules.title: Polyether vs Polyester Distinction
crossValidationRules.rule: Both show BROAD intense C-O @ 1000-1300 cm⁻¹. KEY DIFFERENCE: Polyester has STRONG C=O @ 1700-1730 cm⁻¹. Polyether has NO C=O. Use C=O presence to distinguish. Polyethers used in epoxy resins, polyols, and soft-segment polyurethanes.

startingPoints: Always look for O-H or N-H stretches first (3000-3700 cm⁻¹). These indicate major functional groups.
For INORGANIC FILLERS: Look for single diagnostic O-H (Mg(OH)₂) or multiple O-H peaks (Al(OH)₃). Check secondary metal-O stretches.
Check for C=O (1630-1800 cm⁻¹). Position of C=O tells you what type (aldehyde, ketone, ester, amide, acid).
Look at C-H region (2800-3100 cm⁻¹). Position above/below 3000 indicates saturation/unsaturation.
Examine fingerprint region (400-1500 cm⁻¹) for specific structural features (aromatic, polymer type, fillers).
fillerIdentificationGuide: **Mg(OH)₂**: Single sharp peak @ 3698 cm⁻¹ (FWHM < 25). NO OTHER FILLER shows this.
**Al(OH)₃**: Three sharp O-H peaks @ 3620, 3530, 3400 cm⁻¹. Sharp (FWHM < 40) vs boehmite's broad.
**Boehmite**: Two broad O-H peaks @ 3404, 3089 cm⁻¹. PLUS Al-O stretches @ 1066, 1156 cm⁻¹.
**SiO₂**: Intense broad Si-O @ 1000-1100 cm⁻¹ + sharp @ 800 cm⁻¹. NO O-H.
**Al₂O₃**: Broad Al-O @ 500-900 cm⁻¹. NO O-H stretch. Indicates full calcination.
resinIdentificationGuide: **Epoxy**: Multiplet @ 1250 (C-O-C), 913-917 (C-O def), 830-863 cm⁻¹. Monitor cure by tracking epoxy peaks → O-H @ 3300-3400
**Polyimide (PI)**: DOUBLET @ 1780 + 1720 cm⁻¹ (imide C=O) + C-N @ 1372-1396. Highly diagnostic. High thermal stability.
**Bismaleimide (BMI)**: DOUBLET @ 1776-1765 + 1718-1710 cm⁻¹. Slightly higher than PI (ring strain). C=C visible 1600-1650.
**Polyurethane**: Isocyanate @ 2268 cm⁻¹ (BROAD, STRONG). N-H @ 3300-3400 + C=O @ 1700-1710 (urethane). Monitor cure: NCO decreases, urethane increases.
**Cyanate Ester**: O-C≡N @ 2250-2260 cm⁻¹ (narrow, medium). Disappears during cure (→ aromatic C=N 1500-1600). Aerospace applications.
**Thiol (S-H)**: WEAK SHARP peak @ 2550-2600 cm⁻¹. Used in thiol-epoxy/thiol-yne click chemistry. Rare.
**Polyether**: Broad intense C-O @ 1000-1300 cm⁻¹. NO C=O @ 1700 (distinguishes from polyester). Soft-segment polyol.
commonMistakes: Confusing Al(OH)₃ (sharp) with Boehmite (broad) - check FWHM and exact wavenumbers.
Missing SiO₂ because Si-O symmetric peak @ 800 is small - always look for it.
Assuming broad O-H is only water - compare to Mg(OH)₂ diagnostic single peak.
Not recognizing Al₂O₃ absence of O-H - this is THE diagnostic feature vs hydroxides.
Confusing Isocyanate (N=C=O, 2250-2280, broad) with Alkyne (C≡C, 2100-2260, sharp) - check FWHM!
Missing Epoxy peaks in fingerprint - always check multiplet @ 830-1250 cm⁻¹.
Assuming all doublets @ 1700-1780 are the same - check exact wavenumbers: PI @ 1780+1720, BMI @ 1776-1765+1718-1710.

fillerDatabase.description: Inorganic filler rules for CCL (chlorinated polyethylene) and epoxy formulations

fillers.name: Aluminum Hydroxide
fillers.distinguishFrom: Boehmite - use FWHM and exact wavenumbers

fillers.name: Silica
fillers.noOH: Absence of O-H stretch confirms anhydrous SiO₂

fillers.name: Aluminum Oxide
fillers.note: This 'no O-H' feature distinguishes from hydroxide precursors
fillers.origin: Calcined from boehmite or gibbsite precursors

resinDatabase.description: Thermosetting resin functional groups and identification rules (v3.0.1)

resins.name: Polyurethane (Isocyanate-based)
resins.cureTracking: NCO @ 2268 cm⁻¹ disappears; N-H @ 3300-3400 and C=O @ 1700-1710 appear

resins.name: Cyanate Ester
resins.cureBehavior: Cyanate peak @ 2250-2260 disappears; replaced by aromatic C=N @ 1500-1600 (triazine formation)

resins.name: Thiol-based Resin
resins.use: Thiol-epoxy, thiol-yne systems for mild-condition curing
resins.notes: Less common than isocyanate or epoxy. Used when mild conditions required.

resins.name: Polyether (Polyol)
resins.use: Soft-segment in polyurethanes, epoxy resin matrix modifier
resins.diagnosticFeature: BROAD INTENSE C-O @ 1000-1300 cm⁻¹ with NO C=O @ 1700
resins.distinctions: Polyether has no C=O; polyester has strong C=O @ 1700-1730

cureMonitoringGuide.epoxy: Track epoxy @ 913-1250 cm⁻¹ decrease and O-H @ 3300-3400 cm⁻¹ increase. Degree of cure ∝ O-H increase.
cureMonitoringGuide.isocyanate: Track NCO @ 2268 cm⁻¹ decrease and urethane C=O @ 1700-1710 cm⁻¹ increase. NCO disappearance = full conversion.
cureMonitoringGuide.cyanate: Track OCN @ 2250-2260 cm⁻¹ decrease and triazine aromatic C=N @ 1500-1600 cm⁻¹ increase.
cureMonitoringGuide.bmi: Track C=C @ 1600-1650 cm⁻¹ decrease (Diels-Alder reaction). Full conversion when C=C disappears.
cureMonitoringGuide.pi: Imide peaks @ 1780 and 1720 stabilize once polyamic acid is fully imidized. Track by comparing to precursor.
